recommendations.title: Another Me
recommendations.overview: A teenager finds her perfect life upended when she's stalked by a mysterious doppelganger who has her eyes set on assuming her identity.

recommendations.title: My Life Without Me
recommendations.overview: A fatally ill mother with only two months to live creates a list of things she wants to do before she dies without telling her family of her illness.

recommendations.title: The Secret Life of Words
recommendations.overview: A solitary nurse bonds with a badly burned patient who survived an accident on an oil rig.

recommendations.title: The Bookshop
recommendations.overview: Set in a small English town in 1959, a woman decides, against polite but ruthless local opposition, to open a bookshop, a decision which becomes a political minefield.

recommendations.title: Learning to Drive
recommendations.overview: As her marriage dissolves, a Manhattan writer takes driving lessons from a Sikh instructor with marriage troubles of his own. In each other's company they find the courage to get back on the road and the strength to take the wheel.

recommendations.title: Elisa & Marcela
recommendations.overview: The film tells the true story of Elisa Sánchez Loriga and Marcela Gracia Ibeas, two women in 1901 Spain who fell deeply in love. To overcome societal and legal barriers to their relationship, Elisa assumes a male identity, enabling the pair to marry

recommendations.title: Life in a Year
recommendations.overview: A 17 year old finds out that his girlfriend is dying, so he sets out to give her an entire life, in the last year she has left.

recommendations.title: Nimona
recommendations.genres: Animation, Family, Adventure, Fantasy, Comedy, Action, Drama, Science Fiction
recommendations.overview: A knight framed for a tragic crime teams with a scrappy, shape-shifting teen to prove his innocence.

recommendations.title: Close Range Love
recommendations.overview: A smart high school student falls in love with her English teacher after she receives one-on-one tutoring from him.

recommendations.title: Paris Je T'aime
recommendations.overview: Olivier Assayas, Gus Van Sant, Wes Craven and Alfonso Cuaron are among the 20 distinguished directors who contribute to this collection of 18 stories, each exploring a different aspect of Parisian life. The colourful characters in this drama include
recommendations.director: Gurinder Chadha, Daniela Thomas, Joel Coen, Frédéric Auburtin, Gérard Depardieu, Nobuhiro Suwa, Richard LaGravenese, Bruno Podalydès, Vincenzo Natali, Walter Salles, Ethan Coen, Alfonso Cuarón, Alexander Payne, Tom Tykwer, Isabel Coixet, Oliver Schmitz, Christopher Doyle, Olivier Assayas, Sylvain Chomet, Gus Van Sant, Wes Craven
